summary.text: The dominant pattern today is a massive shift in developer attention toward MCP / Tool Calling, which averaged 1,284 daily stars per repo versus just 17 for Local LLM / Inference — a 75x gap. Puppeteer continues its sustained hot streak with 106 daily stars, while the sudden breakout of CCPM (15x acceleration, +5 stars in 24h) signals growing interest in structured multi-agent project management. The Hacker News top post asking about replacing Claude/GPT with local models (1,055 votes) suggests developer curiosity about local inference remains high, but GitHub activity tells a different story: Local LLM repos are quiet. The absence of any cross-source convergence (0 crossSourceCount) indicates attention is fragmented across platforms, with GitHub favoring tool-calling and browser automation while HN debates local models. If this divergence persists, it may indicate that developers are more interested in building with existing APIs than running models locally.

projects.name: puppeteer
projects.description: JavaScript API for Chrome and Firefox

llmSummary.oneLiner: Puppeteer is a JavaScript library providing a high-level API to control Chrome or Firefox via DevTools Protocol or WebDriver BiDi, primarily used for browser automation and testing.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Automates browser tasks like web scraping, UI testing, and performance monitoring without a visible UI, simplifying headless browser control.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 94,790 stars and a 24-hour star delta of 106, Puppeteer shows sustained high attention. Its acceleration is low (1), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. The project has recent releases and commits, maintaining its position as a top-tier automation tool.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent updates include support for WebDriver BiDi and an experimental WebMCP API, along with a new MCP server (chrome-devtools-mcp) for AI-driven browser automation.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Puppeteer remains essential for developers needing reliable browser automation, and its integration with MCP (Model Context Protocol) aligns with the growing trend of AI agents interacting with web browsers.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Puppeteer is a mature, well-maintained library for browser automation. Its new MCP support makes it a key tool for AI developers building agents that need to browse or test web pages programmatically.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Puppeteer's adoption of WebDriver BiDi and MCP signals a shift toward standardized, cross-browser automation protocols. AI developers should leverage Puppeteer for tasks like web data extraction, UI testing, and AI-driven browser control.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Web developers, QA engineers, data scrapers, and AI developers building browser-based agents or automation workflows.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Dependency on Chrome/Firefox binaries can increase install size; install scripts may be blocked by modern package managers, requiring manual browser download. API changes across major versions may break existing scripts.

llmSummary.problemSolved: Cypress solves the pain of flaky, slow, and hard-to-debug browser tests by providing a developer-friendly, all-in-one testing tool that runs in the same event loop as the application, enabling real-time reloads, time travel, and automatic waiting.
llmSummary.whyNow: Cypress continues to be a dominant force in front-end testing, with 50,098 stars and a sustained 86-star gain in the last 24 hours. llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Cypress is a must-know tool for any developer working on web applications. Its ease of setup, rich debugging features, and active development make it a reliable choice for both small projects and enterprise teams.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Cypress represents a stable, well-supported testing foundation that can be integrated into CI/CD pipelines and AI-driven testing workflows. Its component testing capabilities also align with the trend toward modular, testable UI components.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Front-end developers, QA engineers, and teams building web applications who need fast, reliable end-to-end or component testing.

projects.name: ccpm
projects.description: Project management skill system for Agents that uses GitHub Issues and Git worktrees for parallel agent execution.

llmSummary.oneLiner: CCPM is an Agent Skill that turns AI coding agents into structured project managers using GitHub Issues and parallel Git worktrees for spec-driven, multi-agent development.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding sessions lose context, block on sequential tasks, and produce untraceable code. CCPM enforces a PRD→Epic→Issue→Code pipeline with parallel agent execution and full traceability via GitHub Issues.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 8,166 stars, a 7-day average delta of 2.4, and a sudden acceleration to 2.4 (high), CCPM is experiencing a breakout. The 24-hour delta of 4 and 7-day delta of 17 indicate sustained, accelerating interest. This is not a flash spike but a rapidly growing project with strong community signals.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The high acceleration and sudden_breakout classification suggest recent external mentions (e.g., social media, newsletters) or word-of-mouth from early adopters. The README mentions integration with multiple AI harnesses, which may have expanded its reach.
llmSummary.whyTrending: CCPM addresses a critical pain point in AI-assisted coding: context loss and lack of structured project management. Its promise of parallel agent execution and full traceability resonates with developers moving from 'vibe coding' to production-grade AI workflows. The Agent Skills compatibility lowers adoption friction.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: CCPM offers a ready-to-use framework for structured, multi-agent development. Developers can adopt it to improve code quality, reduce context switching, and enable team collaboration with AI agents. It's particularly valuable for teams using Claude Code, Codex, or similar tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift from ad-hoc AI coding to disciplined, spec-driven development. The emphasis on 'no vibe coding' and full traceability suggests that the next frontier for AI coding tools is not just code generation but project management and accountability. Developers should watch for similar patterns in other tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI-assisted developers and teams using agent-based coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, Factory, etc.) who want structured project management, parallel execution, and traceability. Also relevant for open-source maintainers and solo developers managing complex projects.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is early-stage (no recent release, no recent commits within 7 days). The high star count may be driven by hype rather than proven reliability. Dependency on GitHub Issues and `gh` CLI may limit adoption for teams using other platforms. The 'no vibe coding' philosophy may not suit all workflows.

projects.name: ncnn
projects.description: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for the mobile platform

llmSummary.oneLiner: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment, with no third-party runtime dependencies and support for CPU and Vulkan GPU backends.
llmSummary.problemSolved: ncnn enables efficient deployment of deep learning models on resource-constrained devices (phones, embedded systems, edge devices) without heavy runtime dependencies, bridging the gap between training frameworks like PyTorch and production inference on mobile/edge hardware.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project shows a sudden breakout with a 2.3x acceleration ratio (high) and a 7-day average star delta of 4.1, indicating a recent spike in attention. With 23,375 stars and 4,438 forks, it's already a well-established framework, but the acceleration suggests renewed interest, possibly due to the recent release (within 30 days) and active commits.
llmSummary.whatChanged: A recent release (within 30 days) and continuous commits (last push on 2026-06-16) indicate active development. The README highlights new platform support (HarmonyOS, watchOS, tvOS, visionOS) and the pnnx tool for PyTorch/ONNX conversion, which may have attracted new users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ncnn is trending due to its combination of high performance, broad platform support (Android, iOS, macOS, Windows, Linux, WebAssembly, RISC-V, etc.), and integration with popular frameworks like PyTorch and ONNX via pnnx. The recent acceleration likely stems from the release of new platform builds and the growing need for on-device AI inference.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: ncnn is a mature, production-tested framework for deploying models on mobile and edge devices. Developers should consider it for scenarios where low latency, minimal dependencies, and cross-platform support are critical.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, ncnn signals a shift toward efficient on-device inference, especially for mobile and embedded applications. The pnnx tool simplifies model conversion from PyTorch/ONNX, reducing friction. The Vulkan GPU backend offers performance gains on supported hardware.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile app developers, embedded systems engineers, edge AI practitioners, and anyone needing to deploy deep learning models on resource-constrained devices without heavy runtime dependencies.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has a large number of open issues (1,184), which may indicate maintenance challenges or a high volume of user requests. The C++ API may have a steeper learning curve for Python-only developers, though Python bindings are available.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A curated collection of .mdc configuration files that customize Cursor AI editor behavior for specific frameworks, libraries, and workflows.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using Cursor AI often get generic suggestions that don't respect project-specific conventions, architecture, or preferred libraries. This repo provides ready-to-use rule files that inject context (e.g., 'use getSession not getUser for auth') so the AI generates more relevant, consistent code with fewer manual corrections.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 39,842 stars and is accelerating sharply: 30 stars in the last 24 hours, 125 in the last 7 days, with a 7-day average of 16 stars/day and a 3-day average of 10.7 stars/day. The acceleration ratio of 2.8 (high) and the 'sudden_breakout' trend type indicate a recent spike in attention, likely driven by the growing adoption of Cursor AI and the need for project-specific rules.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed 2026-05-30) and includes new sponsorship banners and rule additions, but there is no release or external buzz event. The acceleration likely reflects broader Cursor AI adoption and word-of-mouth within developer communities.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As Cursor AI gains popularity, developers realize that generic AI suggestions fall short for complex projects. This repo fills a practical need by offering a community-maintained library of rules that make Cursor smarter about specific stacks (Next.js, React, Svelte, etc.), reducing friction and improving code quality.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Cursor AI, this repo is a shortcut to better AI suggestions. Drop the relevant .mdc files into your project's .cursor/rules/ directory to instantly align the AI with your tech stack and conventions.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'AI configuration as a shared asset' — teams are treating AI behavior as something that can be version-controlled, reviewed, and optimized just like code. Expect more curated rule collections and possibly a marketplace for AI editor rules.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using Cursor AI editor, especially those working with modern web frameworks (Next.js, React, Svelte, Angular, Astro, etc.) who want more accurate and context-aware AI code generation.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The rules are community-contributed and may become outdated as frameworks evolve. Some rules may conflict or be overly specific. No official Cursor endorsement. The repo's quality depends on ongoing maintenance.

projects.name: Agent-Reach
projects.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach gives AI agents zero-API-fee access to Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more via a single CLI — no configuration needed for most platforms.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf) cannot natively read or search most web platforms because they lack API keys, face paywalls, or get blocked by anti-bot measures. Agent Reach solves this by installing and configuring free, open-source tools for each platform, handling authentication, fallback routing, and health checks automatically.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo exploded from 0 to 30,716 stars in a very short time, with a 24h delta of 1,738 stars and a 7-day average of 1,105 stars per day. The acceleration ratio of 2.1 indicates a sudden breakout — likely driven by a viral post or community recommendation. This is one of the fastest-growing AI infrastructure projects on GitHub right now.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README mentions a recent update (2026-06) where yt-dlp was replaced with bili-cli for Bilibili due to anti-bot changes, and the project has a recent release within 30 days. The sudden breakout pattern suggests a viral moment (e.g., HN, Twitter, or Reddit post) that accelerated adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: the inability to access web content without paid APIs or complex setup. The promise of 'zero API fees' and 'one CLI' resonates strongly with developers who want their agents to be truly autonomous. The project is also well-documented, supports multiple languages, and has a clear design philosophy.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Agent Reach is a must-try for anyone building AI agents that need web access. It dramatically reduces the friction of integrating platforms like Twitter, Reddit, and YouTube. The architecture (channel-based fallback routing) is a smart pattern for building resilient agent tooling.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'agent infrastructure' as a new category: tools that bridge the gap between AI agents and the web. The success of Agent Reach suggests that developers are willing to adopt opinionated, pre-configured stacks over DIY solutions. The multi-backend fallback pattern is likely to become standard in agent tooling.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers, Claude Code/Cursor/OpenClaw users, anyone building autonomous agents that need to read/search the web, and developers tired of managing API keys and scraping infrastructure.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party tools (twitter-cli, bili-cli, OpenCLI, etc.) that may break or be abandoned. Cookie-based authentication for Twitter and Xiaohongshu carries account ban risk. The rapid growth may attract maintenance challenges. The README explicitly warns about using dedicated accounts for cookie-based platforms.

llmSummary.problemSolved: It eliminates the complexity of managing multiple Kubernetes clusters and cloud native infrastructure by offering a unified control plane with visual design, policy enforcement, and lifecycle management, reducing reliance on YAML and manual configuration.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 10,738 stars, a 24-hour star delta of 284, and a sustained_hot trend type, Meshery is experiencing consistent, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating organic, steady interest rather than a sudden spike. The project has a recent release and commits within 7 days, signaling active maintenance and community engagement.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The sustained_hot trend and high star delta suggest ongoing community adoption and possibly recent feature releases or integrations that maintain momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As a CNCF project, Meshery addresses the growing need for platform engineering and internal developer platforms. Its visual GitOps approach, support for 380+ integrations, and focus on multi-cluster management resonate with teams adopting Kubernetes at scale.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Meshery is a comprehensive tool for developers and platform engineers looking to simplify Kubernetes management, enforce policies, and enable collaborative infrastructure design. Its extensibility and active community make it a strong candidate for adoption.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Meshery's visual design and policy engine (OPA-based) offer opportunities to integrate AI-driven configuration validation and automated remediation workflows, aligning with the trend toward AI-assisted infrastructure management.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Platform engineers, DevOps teams, SREs, and Kubernetes operators managing multi-cluster, multi-cloud environments who need a visual, policy-driven management plane.
llmSummary.riskNotes: High number of open issues (1,514) may indicate maintenance challenges or feature backlog. The project's broad scope could lead to complexity in onboarding and configuration.

llmSummary.problemSolved: Cypress solves the pain of flaky, slow, and hard-to-debug browser tests by providing a developer-friendly, all-in-one testing tool that runs in the same event loop as the application, enabling real-time reloads, time travel, and automatic waiting.
llmSummary.whyNow: Cypress continues to be a dominant force in front-end testing, with 50,098 stars and a sustained 86-star gain in the last 24 hours. The project shows consistent, organic growth without sudden spikes, indicating a mature, widely-adopted tool that remains essential for modern web development.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The latest release is within 30 days, and there are recent commits, but no external buzz or sudden acceleration is detected.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Cypress is trending because it has become the de facto standard for end-to-end testing in the JavaScript ecosystem, with strong community support, extensive integrations (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte), and a cloud offering that adds value for teams.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Cypress is a must-know tool for any developer working on web applications. Its ease of setup, rich debugging features, and active development make it a reliable choice for both small projects and enterprise teams.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Cypress represents a stable, well-supported testing foundation that can be integrated into CI/CD pipelines and AI-driven testing workflows. Its component testing capabilities also align with the trend toward modular, testable UI components.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Front-end developers, QA engineers, and teams building web applications who need fast, reliable end-to-end or component testing.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Cypress has a large open issue count (1,170), which may indicate some maintenance burden or feature requests. Additionally, its cloud offering is a paid service, which could be a consideration for budget-conscious teams.

watchlistMovements.name: Agent-Reach
watchlistMovements.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach gives AI agents zero-API-fee access to Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more via a single CLI — no configuration needed for most platforms.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf) cannot natively read or search most web platforms because they lack API keys, face paywalls, or get blocked by anti-bot measures. Agent Reach solves this by installing and configuring free, open-source tools for each platform, handling authentication, fallback routing, and health checks automatically.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo exploded from 0 to 30,716 stars in a very short time, with a 24h delta of 1,738 stars and a 7-day average of 1,105 stars per day. The acceleration ratio of 2.1 indicates a sudden breakout — likely driven by a viral post or community recommendation. This is one of the fastest-growing AI infrastructure projects on GitHub right now.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README mentions a recent update (2026-06) where yt-dlp was replaced with bili-cli for Bilibili due to anti-bot changes, and the project has a recent release within 30 days. The sudden breakout pattern suggests a viral moment (e.g., HN, Twitter, or Reddit post) that accelerated adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: the inability to access web content without paid APIs or complex setup. The promise of 'zero API fees' and 'one CLI' resonates strongly with developers who want their agents to be truly autonomous. The project is also well-documented, supports multiple languages, and has a clear design philosophy.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Agent Reach is a must-try for anyone building AI agents that need web access. It dramatically reduces the friction of integrating platforms like Twitter, Reddit, and YouTube. The architecture (channel-based fallback routing) is a smart pattern for building resilient agent tooling.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'agent infrastructure' as a new category: tools that bridge the gap between AI agents and the web. The success of Agent Reach suggests that developers are willing to adopt opinionated, pre-configured stacks over DIY solutions. The multi-backend fallback pattern is likely to become standard in agent tooling.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers, Claude Code/Cursor/OpenClaw users, anyone building autonomous agents that need to read/search the web, and developers tired of managing API keys and scraping infrastructure.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party tools (twitter-cli, bili-cli, OpenCLI, etc.) that may break or be abandoned. Cookie-based authentication for Twitter and Xiaohongshu carries account ban risk. The rapid growth may attract maintenance challenges. The README explicitly warns about using dedicated accounts for cookie-based platforms.

modelDemoSignals.title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
modelDemoSignals.description: We investigate whether language models internally track the value of their current trajectory, defined as the likelihood that their ongoing strategy will achieve their goals. Using synthetic, in-context reinforcement learning data, we construct a "value" axis for Qwen3-8B. We find that activations along this axis distinguish between high vs. low verbalized confidence, rollouts without and with backtracking, and correct vs. corrupted code. Steering towards high value causally suppresses self-corr
modelDemoSignals.summary: We investigate whether language models internally track the value of their current trajectory, defined as the likelihood that their ongoing strategy will achieve their goals. Using synthetic, in-context reinforcement learning data, we construct a "value" axis for Qwen3-8B. We find that activations along this axis distinguish between high vs. low verbalized confidence, rollouts without and with backtracking, and correct vs. corrupted code. Steering towards high value causally suppresses self-correction and reduces explanatory verbosity, while steering towards low value induces backtracking and exploration. We demonstrate that direct preference optimization (DPO) can increase the internal value of rewarded behaviors (e.g. use a certain word), causing the model to act more confidently after exhibiting them. Finally, we apply the value axis to study in-the-wild settings. For example, we find that Qwen assigns low value to politically sensitive chat queries after post-training and that supervised fine-tuning increases internal confidence within the training domain. Our results suggest that language models linearly encode an estimate of expected goal success that modulates their confidence in pursuing a direction.
modelDemoSignals.author: Nick Jiang, Isaac Kauvar, Jack Lindsey

modelDemoSignals.title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
modelDemoSignals.description: Large language models (LLMs) often fail when answering requires identifying a small but decisive piece of evidence within a long or complex context, such as a single line in a tool trace or a subtle detail in an image. We propose ContextRL, a context-aware reinforcement learning (RL) method that improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance through an \emph{indirect} auxiliary objective. Instead of supervising only the final answer, ContextRL presents the model with a query, an answ
modelDemoSignals.summary: Large language models (LLMs) often fail when answering requires identifying a small but decisive piece of evidence within a long or complex context, such as a single line in a tool trace or a subtle detail in an image. We propose ContextRL, a context-aware reinforcement learning (RL) method that improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance through an \emph{indirect} auxiliary objective. Instead of supervising only the final answer, ContextRL presents the model with a query, an answer, and two highly similar contexts, and rewards it for selecting the context that supports the query--answer pair, thereby encouraging fine-grained grounding. We construct contrastive context data in two domains: for coding agents, trajectories serve as contexts, yielding 1k pairs built via condition filtering; for multimodal reasoning, images serve as contexts, yielding 7K pairs built via generative editing and similarity search. ContextRL achieves average gains of +2.2% over standard GRPO on 5 long-horizon benchmarks, and +1.8% across 12 diverse visual question answering benchmarks. To disentangle the effect of the proposed objective from that of additional data, we compare against data-augmentation baselines that repurpose the same contrastive contexts as standard query--context--answer examples. These baselines provide little to no improvement, showing that the gains arise from the proposed context-selection objective rather than from the contrastive data alone.
modelDemoSignals.author: Peiyang Xu, Bangzheng Li, Sijia Liu, Karthik R. Narasimhan, Pramod Viswanath, Prateek Mittal, Xingyu Fu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, rag, coding agent

modelDemoSignals.title: Exact Posterior Score Estimation for Solving Linear Inverse Problems
modelDemoSignals.description: Diffusion and flow-based models learn powerful data priors by training a denoiser to reverse Gaussian corruption. To use this prior to solve a linear inverse problem, one needs to sample from the posterior, but the score that the prior provides is the unconditional score, not the posterior score. Existing methods either steer a fixed pretrained denoiser with approximate measurement-matching corrections, or train a conditional restoration model that abandons the denoising structure of the prior.
modelDemoSignals.summary: Diffusion and flow-based models learn powerful data priors by training a denoiser to reverse Gaussian corruption. To use this prior to solve a linear inverse problem, one needs to sample from the posterior, but the score that the prior provides is the unconditional score, not the posterior score. Existing methods either steer a fixed pretrained denoiser with approximate measurement-matching corrections, or train a conditional restoration model that abandons the denoising structure of the prior. We derive the exact posterior score in closed form for linear Gaussian inverse problems under general Gaussian interpolants, and show that posterior sampling reduces to a denoising problem at an operator-dependent shifted pivot under an anisotropic noise covariance. We turn this identity into Exact Posterior Score (EPS), a denoising training objective that preserves the input/output structure of standard pretraining and can therefore be trained from scratch or fine-tuned from a pretrained denoiser. At inference, EPS uses the same sampler as the underlying backbone, with no likelihood gradients or projections. We evaluate EPS on five linear inverse problems across FFHQ and ImageNet, where it outperforms training-free and training-based baselines on fidelity, perceptual, and distributional metrics, while using roughly an order of magnitude fewer denoiser evaluations than gradient-based posterior samplers.
modelDemoSignals.author: Abbas Mammadov, Ozgur Kara, Kaan Oktay, Iskander Azangulov, Adil Kaan Akan, Hyungjin Chung, James Matthew Rehg, Yee Whye Teh

modelDemoSignals.title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
modelDemoSignals.description: Generalist robot policies must follow user instructions while reasoning about how objects, cameras, and robot actions interact in the 3D physical world. Recent vision-language-action models (VLAs) and video world-action models (WAMs) inherit strong semantic or temporal priors from large-scale foundation models, but they still operate primarily on 2D image frames or 2D-derived latent spaces, leaving implicit the 3D geometry required for contact-rich manipulation. We propose the Geometric Action M
modelDemoSignals.summary: Generalist robot policies must follow user instructions while reasoning about how objects, cameras, and robot actions interact in the 3D physical world. Recent vision-language-action models (VLAs) and video world-action models (WAMs) inherit strong semantic or temporal priors from large-scale foundation models, but they still operate primarily on 2D image frames or 2D-derived latent spaces, leaving implicit the 3D geometry required for contact-rich manipulation. We propose the Geometric Action Model (GAM), a language-conditioned manipulation policy that directly repurposes a pretrained geometric foundation model (GFM) as a shared substrate for perception, temporal prediction, and action decoding. GAM splits the GFM at an intermediate layer: the shallow layers serve as an observation encoder, and a causal future predictor inserted at the split layer forecasts future latent tokens conditioned on language, proprioception, and action history. The predicted future tokens are then routed through the remaining GFM blocks for feature propagation and decoding, allowing a single backbone to produce both future geometry and actions. This design equips the GFM with language-conditioned temporal world modeling through minimal architectural modification while preserving its rich geometric priors. Across a broad suite of simulation and real-robot manipulation benchmarks, GAM is more accurate, more robust, faster, and lighter than current foundation-model-scale baselines.
modelDemoSignals.author: Jisang Han, Seonghu Jeon, Jaewoo Jung, René Zurbrügg, Honggyu An, Tifanny Portela, Marco Hutter, Marc Pollefeys, Seungryong Kim, Sunghwan Hong

modelDemoSignals.title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes

Existing approaches commonly reduce this sparse outcome to a single scalar reward or advantage signal, which conflates distinct forms of transition-level feedback and provides limited guidance once basic task success becomes achievable. modelDemoSignals.summary: When pretrained VLA policies are fine-tuned through online RL, each rollout episode produces only a single binary outcome (success or failure), yet the actor update requires per-transition supervision. Existing approaches commonly reduce this sparse outcome to a single scalar reward or advantage signal, which conflates distinct forms of transition-level feedback and provides limited guidance once basic task success becomes achievable. First, a single scalar signal conflates the two objectives of viability and efficiency; once basic success is achieved, the binary label provides no gradient to distinguish efficient completions from slow ones. Second, real-world rollouts mix autonomous and intervention segments; naively assigning episode outcomes across these boundaries introduces incorrect credit assignment. To address these issues, we propose Hierarchical Advantage-Weighted Behavior Cloning (HABC), which trains separate critic heads for these two objectives on different data subsets and combines their outputs with a state-adaptive balance. A state-adaptive gate $g_t$ merges their one-step advantages, prioritizing viability when success is uncertain and shifting to efficiency only when viability is high, and converts the result into per-transition weights on the actor loss. Intervention-aware credit assignment further restricts outcome labels to segments executed by the current policy, preventing supervision from leaking across intervention boundaries. In real-robot experiments on three contact-rich bimanual tasks, HABC raises success from supervised fine-tuning (SFT) baselines of 36%, 44%, and 12% to 92%, 88%, and 38%.
modelDemoSignals.author: Tongyan Fang, Siyuan Huang, Naiyu Fang, Ganlong Zhao, Zhongjin Luo, Jianbo Liu, Xiaogang Wang, Ying Dong, Hongsheng Li

aihotHighlights.title: DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，估值超 500 亿美元
aihotHighlights.summary: 中国 AI 初创公司 DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，募资超 500 亿元人民币（约 74 亿美元），估值超 500 亿美元。投资结构特殊：多数投资者将资金投入 CEO 梁文锋管理的有限合伙企业，无投票权且锁定期五年；仅国资 AI 基金直接投资并保留投票权。创始人梁文锋个人投入约 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代为主要外部投资者。梁文锋表示优先基础 AI 研究与 AGI 开发，将继续开源。DeepSeek 去年初凭 V3、R1 模型获全球关注，今年 4 月发布运行于华为芯片的最大开源权重模型 V4，并将 V4 Pro 永久折扣 75%，输入价格约为 OpenAI GPT-5.5 的 1/11，输出价格约为 1/35。

aihotHighlights.title: Google Cloud 推出 OKF v0.1：供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文
aihotHighlights.summary: Google Cloud 发布 Open Knowledge Format （OKF） v0.1，一种供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文知识。OKF 将知识表示为带 YAML 前置元数据的 markdown 文件目录，每个概念对应一个文件，通过 `type`、`title`、`description` 等少量保留字段实现互操作。无需专有服务、SDK 或运行时，目录可托管在 GitHub、以 tarball 传输或挂载到任意文件系统。OKF 旨在解决组织内部知识碎片化问题--表结构、指标定义、runbook 等散落在不同 catalog 和 wiki 中，各厂商方案互不兼容。遵循最少意见原则，只强制 `type` 字段，生产者和消费者可独立实现。使用场景包括数据团队将 BigQuery 表定义导出为代码、为智能体存储 incident runbook、跨组织知识交换等。

aihotHighlights.title: Cartesia 发布 Sonic 3.5 与 Ink 2 实时语音模型
aihotHighlights.summary: Cartesia 推出 Sonic 3.5 和 Ink 2 两个模型，作为单一实时语音栈，分别负责文本转语音和语音转文本。Ink 2 在 Artificial Analysis 的流式语音转文字排行榜上排名第一。Sonic 3.5 在实时文本转语音中位列榜首，首音频延迟约 82ms。Cartesia 成为目前唯一同时拥有 #1 听与说模型的提供商。

aihotHighlights.title: AI 版支付宝官宣开启邀测：右滑打开"阿宝"，官方放出 100 个邀请码
aihotHighlights.summary: 支付宝今日开启 AI 版邀测，用户可右滑进入新版界面，在对话框或语音中输入指令，由"阿宝"助手代办事项。以查询公积金为例，阿宝自动匹配对应小程序和服务入口，用户点击确认即可完成，将多步跳转折叠为一句指令。所有涉及资金变动和支付的环节均需用户本人确认。首批放出 100 个邀请码。

aihotHighlights.title: 毕业生陷入AI检测荒诞循环：手写摘要被判99%AI率，AI写部分0%
aihotHighlights.summary: 当前毕业生面临论文AIGC率检测荒诞困境。学生手写摘要被判定99%AI率，纯AI写部分却为0%。学校要求AIGC率不超40%，学生用Claude反复修改并花上百元检测费（维普20元/篇，知网/万方2元/千字符），最终降至36.1%。答辩时老师要求改回学术表达，AI率回升至37.21%。同一论文在不同平台检测结果差异巨大（48%、44%、59%）。部分平台提供降重收费服务，少数学校已改用AI使用声明表替代一刀切检测。

aihotHighlights.title: 伯克利RDI发布Agents' Last Exam基准
aihotHighlights.summary: 2026年6月，伯克利RDI发布Agents' Last Exam（ALE）基准，包含1，500余项源于真实工作的任务，覆盖55个非体力职业。对Fable 5、GPT-5.5、Composer 2.5等前沿智能体的测评显示：在最困难层级成功率均为0%；整体任务表现接近，但单任务成本差异巨大（Fable 5约$15.70，GPT-5.5约$3.80，Composer 2.5约$1.33）。CLI子集ALE-CLI最佳通过率仅25.2%。主要失败模式是智能体未验证输出即宣称完成。数据集、代码及CLI子集已开源。

aihotHighlights.title: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统全新升级上线：智能筛选志愿，数据权威可信
aihotHighlights.summary: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统今日全新升级上线，依托招生、学籍、就业等海量官方数据，免费为考生和家长提供志愿填报服务。系统支持31个省区市本专科普通批次志愿筛选，输入高考成绩、位次及个性化条件即可快速生成合理参考方案。AI助手"智慧小招"24小时在线解答政策规则。平台数据由高校直接报送、官方核验，真实可靠。同时推出专业倾向测评和21项生涯测评工具，帮助学生认清特长、规划未来。

aihotHighlights.title: 成本砍半，字节跳动推出 Seedance 2.0 Mini 视频生成模型
aihotHighlights.summary: 字节跳动火山引擎旗下火山方舟体验中心于 6 月 15 日上线 Seedance 2.0 Mini 视频生成模型，计划近期开放 API。该模型比 Seedance 2.0 Fast 快 2 倍，输出质量相当。图生视频定价 0.023 元/千 tokens，视频生视频 0.014 元/千 tokens，720P 规格下单秒生成成本约 0.5 元，较 Seedance 2.0 标准版降低约一半。模型面向电商内容生产、营销素材批量生成、UGC 创作及特效玩法等高频率、大规模视频生成场景。

aihotHighlights.title: 五角大楼将大部分日常AI工作流从Anthropic转移，目标9月前完全切断
aihotHighlights.summary: 五角大楼宣布已将超2/3日常AI工作流从Anthropic转移，目标9月前清零。起因是年初五角大楼要求Anthropic签署协议允许Claude用于大规模监控和全自动武器，CEO Dario Amodei以模型不可靠为由拒绝。五角大楼将其列为"供应链风险"，起诉未果。OpenAI调整立场获得订单。Polymarket预测6月底前和解概率仅9%。此事件凸显AI公司需在原则与政府合作间抉择。

aihotHighlights.title: Qwen-RobotManip：对齐解锁机器人操作基础模型的规模化能力
aihotHighlights.summary: Qwen-RobotManip 是通义千问基于 Qwen-VL 的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）基础模型，引入覆盖表示、运动和行为三维度的统一对齐框架。仅使用开源机器人数据集和人演示视频，构建约 38，100 小时预训练语料，涵盖 15 种机器人形态。在 LIBERO-Plus 达 91.4%，RoboTwin-C2R Hard 达 69.4%，RoboCasa365 Composite-Unseen 达 14.9%，EBench 达 45.6%，RoboTwin-IF 达 72.0%，并在 RoboChallenge Table30 v1 generalist track 夺冠。模型采用 80 维状态-动作表示、人-机器人数据合成管道（1，933 小时第一人称视频转 24，808 小时数据）及上下文策略适配。

aihotHighlights.title: Qwen-RobotWorld：具身智能体的无界世界
aihotHighlights.summary: Qwen-RobotWorld以语言为统一动作接口，采用双流Multimodal Diffusion Transformer（MMDiT）架构，将Qwen2.5-VL作为动作编码器。在4个基准测试中取得顶尖成绩，统一20余种机器人形态，基于860万跨场景训练对和1300多项操作技能。语言接口标准化500多种动作类别，支持操作、自动驾驶、室内导航的联合训练。还支持Scene2Robot人类到机器人转移及2-4路多视角几何一致视频生成。

aihotHighlights.title: Qwen-RobotNav：面向智能体导航系统的可扩展导航模型
aihotHighlights.summary: Qwen 发布 Qwen-RobotNav，基于 Qwen3-VL 在 15.6M 样本上训练，统一了视觉语言导航、目标导航、目标跟踪、自动驾驶和具身问答五个领域，无需修改架构即可在推理时切换任务模式和观察参数。模型在多项基准取得 SOTA：VLN-CE RxR 成功率 76.5%，HM3Dv2 目标导航 75.6%（仅 RGB），EVT-Bench 跟踪率 90.0%，NAVSIM PDMS 91.4，以及三项 EQA 新标杆。模型暴露四个可调轴（视觉 token 预算、时间衰减、相机权重、帧采样模式）。作为智能体系统的一部分，上层规划器 Qwen3.7-Plus 在 EXPRESS-Bench 上提升 15.4%，导航步数减少 77%。模型已零样本部署在 Unitree Go2 四足机器人上，无需环境微调。

aihotHighlights.title: 项目负责人揭秘为何苹果 AI 版 Siri 姗姗来迟：推倒重来，彻底重构
aihotHighlights.summary: 苹果AI版Siri迟迟未上线，项目负责人迈克·罗克韦尔在WWDC技术分享会上透露，去年团队曾做出在原有Siri基础上小幅改良、新增工具调用的可运行版本，但因无法达到产品愿景，最终选择推倒重来，完整从零重构系统，依托全新大模型搭建。重构后的Siri拥有独立应用程序，原生支持多模态交互，隐私保护贯穿底层架构，并覆盖iPhone、iPad、Mac、Apple Watch、Vision Pro、CarPlay、AirPods等全平台，提供统一连贯体验。

aihotHighlights.title: Meta 在 Facebook 上线"AI Mode"，基于平台公开信息合成答案
aihotHighlights.summary: Meta 宣布在 Facebook 推出"AI Mode"搜索功能，利用 Meta AI 从公开帖子（含群组和 Reels）提取信息并合成答案，用户可用自然语言提问获得摘要。同时新增视频拼贴剪辑、过渡效果及 AI 照片预设（可更换服装、发型和配饰），体育迷可在 Stories 中点击"AI Edit"虚拟穿上队服。这些更新延续了此前动态头像、Marketplace 自动回复和创作者 AI 助手的部署节奏。此外，Meta 近期启动了 Facebook、Instagram 和 WhatsApp 的全球订阅计划（每月 3.99 美元起），更多 AI 订阅层级正在规划中。

items.title: DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，估值超 500 亿美元
items.description: 中国 AI 初创公司 DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，募资超 500 亿元人民币（约 74 亿美元），估值超 500 亿美元。投资结构特殊：多数投资者将资金投入 CEO 梁文锋管理的有限合伙企业，无投票权且锁定期五年；仅国资 AI 基金直接投资并保留投票权。创始人梁文锋个人投入约 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代为主要外部投资者。梁文锋表示优先基础 AI 研究与 AGI 开发，将继续开源。DeepSeek 去年初凭 V3、R1 模型获全球关注，今年 4 月发布运行于华为芯片的最大开源权重模型 V4，并将 V4 Pro 永久折扣 75%，输入价格约为 OpenAI GPT-5.5 的 1/11，输出价格约为 1/35。

items.title: Google Cloud 推出 OKF v0.1：供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文
items.description: Google Cloud 发布 Open Knowledge Format （OKF） v0.1，一种供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文知识。OKF 将知识表示为带 YAML 前置元数据的 markdown 文件目录，每个概念对应一个文件，通过 `type`、`title`、`description` 等少量保留字段实现互操作。无需专有服务、SDK 或运行时，目录可托管在 GitHub、以 tarball 传输或挂载到任意文件系统。OKF 旨在解决组织内部知识碎片化问题--表结构、指标定义、runbook 等散落在不同 catalog 和 wiki 中，各厂商方案互不兼容。遵循最少意见原则，只强制 `type` 字段，生产者和消费者可独立实现。使用场景包括数据团队将 BigQuery 表定义导出为代码、为智能体存储 incident runbook、跨组织知识交换等。

items.title: Cartesia 发布 Sonic 3.5 与 Ink 2 实时语音模型
items.description: Cartesia 推出 Sonic 3.5 和 Ink 2 两个模型，作为单一实时语音栈，分别负责文本转语音和语音转文本。Ink 2 在 Artificial Analysis 的流式语音转文字排行榜上排名第一。Sonic 3.5 在实时文本转语音中位列榜首，首音频延迟约 82ms。Cartesia 成为目前唯一同时拥有 #1 听与说模型的提供商。

items.title: AI 版支付宝官宣开启邀测：右滑打开"阿宝"，官方放出 100 个邀请码
items.description: 支付宝今日开启 AI 版邀测，用户可右滑进入新版界面，在对话框或语音中输入指令，由"阿宝"助手代办事项。以查询公积金为例，阿宝自动匹配对应小程序和服务入口，用户点击确认即可完成，将多步跳转折叠为一句指令。所有涉及资金变动和支付的环节均需用户本人确认。首批放出 100 个邀请码。

items.title: 毕业生陷入AI检测荒诞循环：手写摘要被判99%AI率，AI写部分0%
items.description: 当前毕业生面临论文AIGC率检测荒诞困境。学生手写摘要被判定99%AI率，纯AI写部分却为0%。学校要求AIGC率不超40%，学生用Claude反复修改并花上百元检测费（维普20元/篇，知网/万方2元/千字符），最终降至36.1%。答辩时老师要求改回学术表达，AI率回升至37.21%。同一论文在不同平台检测结果差异巨大（48%、44%、59%）。部分平台提供降重收费服务，少数学校已改用AI使用声明表替代一刀切检测。

items.title: 伯克利RDI发布Agents' Last Exam基准
items.description: 2026年6月，伯克利RDI发布Agents' Last Exam（ALE）基准，包含1，500余项源于真实工作的任务，覆盖55个非体力职业。对Fable 5、GPT-5.5、Composer 2.5等前沿智能体的测评显示：在最困难层级成功率均为0%；整体任务表现接近，但单任务成本差异巨大（Fable 5约$15.70，GPT-5.5约$3.80，Composer 2.5约$1.33）。CLI子集ALE-CLI最佳通过率仅25.2%。主要失败模式是智能体未验证输出即宣称完成。数据集、代码及CLI子集已开源。

items.title: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统全新升级上线：智能筛选志愿，数据权威可信
items.description: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统今日全新升级上线，依托招生、学籍、就业等海量官方数据，免费为考生和家长提供志愿填报服务。系统支持31个省区市本专科普通批次志愿筛选，输入高考成绩、位次及个性化条件即可快速生成合理参考方案。AI助手"智慧小招"24小时在线解答政策规则。平台数据由高校直接报送、官方核验，真实可靠。同时推出专业倾向测评和21项生涯测评工具，帮助学生认清特长、规划未来。

items.title: 成本砍半，字节跳动推出 Seedance 2.0 Mini 视频生成模型
items.description: 字节跳动火山引擎旗下火山方舟体验中心于 6 月 15 日上线 Seedance 2.0 Mini 视频生成模型，计划近期开放 API。该模型比 Seedance 2.0 Fast 快 2 倍，输出质量相当。图生视频定价 0.023 元/千 tokens，视频生视频 0.014 元/千 tokens，720P 规格下单秒生成成本约 0.5 元，较 Seedance 2.0 标准版降低约一半。模型面向电商内容生产、营销素材批量生成、UGC 创作及特效玩法等高频率、大规模视频生成场景。

DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，估值超 500 亿美元
items.description: 中国 AI 初创公司 DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，募资超 500 亿元人民币（约 74 亿美元），估值超 500 亿美元。投资结构特殊：多数投资者将资金投入 CEO 梁文锋管理的有限合伙企业，无投票权且锁定期五年；仅国资 AI 基金直接投资并保留投票权。创始人梁文锋个人投入约 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代为主要外部投资者。梁文锋表示优先基础 AI 研究与 AGI 开发，将继续开源。DeepSeek 去年初凭 V3、R1 模型获全球关注，今年 4 月发布运行于华为芯片的最大开源权重模型 V4，并将 V4 Pro 永久折扣 75%，输入价格约为 OpenAI GPT-5.5 的 1/11，输出价格约为 1/35。

items.summary: Cartesia 推出 Sonic 3.5 和 Ink 2 两个模型，作为单一实时语音栈，分别负责文本转语音和语音转文本。Ink 2 在 Artificial Analysis 的流式语音转文字排行榜上排名第一。Sonic 3.5 在实时文本转语音中位列榜首，首音频延迟约 82ms。Cartesia 成为目前唯一同时拥有 #1 听与说模型的提供商。

trendEntities.summary: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Agent-Reach is an open-source CLI tool that enables AI agents to search and read content from multiple platforms (Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, etc.) without API fees.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained significant star growth (6,936 stars in 7 days, 2,461 in 24h), indicating strong developer interest in multi-platform agent tooling. This aligns with the broader trend of MCP (Model Context Protocol) and tool-calling capabilities for AI agents.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI/ML engineers building agentic systems, developers working on web-scraping or data aggregation tools, product managers exploring agent-based automation.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Enables cost-effective data access for AI agents, potentially reducing reliance on paid APIs. Could be used for market research, social listening, or competitive analysis.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a ready-to-use tool for integrating multi-platform search into agent workflows. Developers can extend or customize the tool for specific use cases.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Rapid star growth may be driven by hype; actual adoption and reliability are unverified. No evidence of production use. Potential legal/ToS issues with scraping platforms.

trendEntities.summary: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before sending them to an LLM, reducing token usage by 60-95% while preserving answer quality. It offers a library, proxy, and MCP server.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained significant GitHub attention (29k+ stars, +14.7k in 7 days), indicating strong developer interest in cost-efficient LLM usage. This aligns with the broader trend of optimizing LLM interactions for production workloads.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building LLM-powered applications, especially those handling large context windows or high-volume API calls; product managers focused on reducing LLM costs; and teams deploying AI agents or RAG systems.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly reduces LLM API costs (by up to 95% token reduction) without sacrificing answer quality, making AI features more economically viable at scale.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a drop-in solution (library, proxy, MCP server) to compress inputs, potentially lowering latency and costs in existing LLM pipelines. Easy to integrate and test.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Compression may lose nuance in edge cases; effectiveness depends on content type; GitHub stars reflect attention, not production validation. No evidence of widespread adoption yet.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Open Design is an open-source, local-first desktop application that serves as an alternative to Claude Design, offering a wide range of design and prototyping capabilities including web, desktop, mobile prototypes, slides, images, and videos, with support for multiple design systems and CLI integrations.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has seen significant star growth (3,926 stars in 7 days, 810 in 24h) and a high heat score of 420, indicating strong developer interest. It positions itself as a local-first alternative to proprietary design tools, which aligns with current trends toward open-source and privacy-focused development tools.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Frontend developers, UI/UX designers, product designers, and developers using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) who need a local-first design tool integrated with their workflow.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Offers a free, open-source alternative to paid design tools, potentially reducing costs for startups and enterprises. Its local-first nature addresses data privacy concerns, which is increasingly important for regulated industries.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Developers can integrate design and prototyping directly into their CLI-based workflows, reducing context switching. The support for multiple AI coding agents and design systems makes it a versatile addition to a developer's toolkit.
llmSummary.riskNotes: High star growth may be driven by initial hype; actual adoption and stability are unproven. As a new project, it may lack maturity, documentation, and community support. Local-first may limit collaboration features compared to cloud-based tools.

trendEntities.title: DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，估值超 500 亿美元
normalizedKeys: title:deepseek 完成首轮外部融资 估值超 500 亿美元

items.title: DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，估值超 500 亿美元
items.summary: 中国 AI 初创公司 DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，募资超 500 亿元人民币（约 74 亿美元），估值超 500 亿美元。投资结构特殊：多数投资者将资金投入 CEO 梁文锋管理的有限合伙企业，无投票权且锁定期五年；仅国资 AI 基金直接投资并保留投票权。创始人梁文锋个人投入约 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代为主要外部投资者。梁文锋表示优先基础 AI 研究与 AGI 开发，将继续开源。DeepSeek 去年初凭 V3、R1 模型获全球关注，今年 4 月发布运行于华为芯片的最大开源权重模型 V4，并将 V4 Pro 永久折扣 75%，输入价格约为 OpenAI GPT-5.5 的 1/11，输出价格约为 1/35。

trendEntities.title: Google Cloud 推出 OKF v0.1：供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文
normalizedKeys: title:google cloud 推出 okf v0 1 供应商中立的 markdown 规范 为 ai 智能体提供结构化上下文

items.title: Google Cloud 推出 OKF v0.1：供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文
items.summary: Google Cloud 发布 Open Knowledge Format （OKF） v0.1，一种供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文知识。OKF 将知识表示为带 YAML 前置元数据的 markdown 文件目录，每个概念对应一个文件，通过 `type`、`title`、`description` 等少量保留字段实现互操作。无需专有服务、SDK 或运行时，目录可托管在 GitHub、以 tarball 传输或挂载到任意文件系统。OKF 旨在解决组织内部知识碎片化问题--表结构、指标定义、runbook 等散落在不同 catalog 和 wiki 中，各厂商方案互不兼容。遵循最少意见原则，只强制 `type` 字段，生产者和消费者可独立实现。使用场景包括数据团队将 BigQuery 表定义导出为代码、为智能体存储 incident runbook、跨组织知识交换等。

trendEntities.title: Cartesia 发布 Sonic 3.5 与 Ink 2 实时语音模型
normalizedKeys: title:cartesia 发布 sonic 3 5 与 ink 2 实时语音模型

items.title: Cartesia 发布 Sonic 3.5 与 Ink 2 实时语音模型
items.summary: Cartesia 推出 Sonic 3.5 和 Ink 2 两个模型，作为单一实时语音栈，分别负责文本转语音和语音转文本。Ink 2 在 Artificial Analysis 的流式语音转文字排行榜上排名第一。Sonic 3.5 在实时文本转语音中位列榜首，首音频延迟约 82ms。Cartesia 成为目前唯一同时拥有 #1 听与说模型的提供商。

trendEntities.title: AI 版支付宝官宣开启邀测：右滑打开"阿宝"，官方放出 100 个邀请码
normalizedKeys: title:ai 版支付宝官宣开启邀测 右滑打开 阿宝 官方放出 100 个邀请码

items.title: AI 版支付宝官宣开启邀测：右滑打开"阿宝"，官方放出 100 个邀请码
items.summary: 支付宝今日开启 AI 版邀测，用户可右滑进入新版界面，在对话框或语音中输入指令，由"阿宝"助手代办事项。以查询公积金为例，阿宝自动匹配对应小程序和服务入口，用户点击确认即可完成，将多步跳转折叠为一句指令。所有涉及资金变动和支付的环节均需用户本人确认。首批放出 100 个邀请码。

trendEntities.title: 毕业生陷入AI检测荒诞循环：手写摘要被判99%AI率，AI写部分0%
normalizedKeys: title:毕业生陷入ai检测荒诞循环 手写摘要被判99 ai率 ai写部分0

items.title: 毕业生陷入AI检测荒诞循环：手写摘要被判99%AI率，AI写部分0%
items.summary: 当前毕业生面临论文AIGC率检测荒诞困境。学生手写摘要被判定99%AI率，纯AI写部分却为0%。学校要求AIGC率不超40%，学生用Claude反复修改并花上百元检测费（维普20元/篇，知网/万方2元/千字符），最终降至36.1%。答辩时老师要求改回学术表达，AI率回升至37.21%。同一论文在不同平台检测结果差异巨大（48%、44%、59%）。部分平台提供降重收费服务，少数学校已改用AI使用声明表替代一刀切检测。

trendEntities.title: 伯克利RDI发布Agents' Last Exam基准
normalizedKeys: title:伯克利rdi发布agents last exam基准

items.title: 伯克利RDI发布Agents' Last Exam基准
items.summary: 2026年6月，伯克利RDI发布Agents' Last Exam（ALE）基准，包含1，500余项源于真实工作的任务，覆盖55个非体力职业。对Fable 5、GPT-5.5、Composer 2.5等前沿智能体的测评显示：在最困难层级成功率均为0%；整体任务表现接近，但单任务成本差异巨大（Fable 5约$15.70，GPT-5.5约$3.80，Composer 2.5约$1.33）。CLI子集ALE-CLI最佳通过率仅25.2%。主要失败模式是智能体未验证输出即宣称完成。数据集、代码及CLI子集已开源。

trendEntities.title: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统全新升级上线：智能筛选志愿，数据权威可信
normalizedKeys: title:教育部 阳光志愿 信息服务系统全新升级上线 智能筛选志愿 数据权威可信

items.title: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统全新升级上线：智能筛选志愿，数据权威可信
items.summary: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统今日全新升级上线，依托招生、学籍、就业等海量官方数据，免费为考生和家长提供志愿填报服务。系统支持31个省区市本专科普通批次志愿筛选，输入高考成绩、位次及个性化条件即可快速生成合理参考方案。AI助手"智慧小招"24小时在线解答政策规则。平台数据由高校直接报送、官方核验，真实可靠。同时推出专业倾向测评和21项生涯测评工具，帮助学生认清特长、规划未来。

trendEntities.title: 成本砍半，字节跳动推出 Seedance 2.0 Mini 视频生成模型
normalizedKeys: title:成本砍半 字节跳动推出 seedance 2 0 mini 视频生成模型

items.title: 成本砍半，字节跳动推出 Seedance 2.0 Mini 视频生成模型
items.summary: 字节跳动火山引擎旗下火山方舟体验中心于 6 月 15 日上线 Seedance 2.0 Mini 视频生成模型，计划近期开放 API。该模型比 Seedance 2.0 Fast 快 2 倍，输出质量相当。图生视频定价 0.023 元/千 tokens，视频生视频 0.014 元/千 tokens，720P 规格下单秒生成成本约 0.5 元，较 Seedance 2.0 标准版降低约一半。模型面向电商内容生产、营销素材批量生成、UGC 创作及特效玩法等高频率、大规模视频生成场景。

trendEntities.title: 五角大楼将大部分日常AI工作流从Anthropic转移，目标9月前完全切断
normalizedKeys: title:五角大楼将大部分日常ai工作流从anthropic转移 目标9月前完全切断

items.title: 五角大楼将大部分日常AI工作流从Anthropic转移，目标9月前完全切断
items.summary: 五角大楼宣布已将超2/3日常AI工作流从Anthropic转移，目标9月前清零。起因是年初五角大楼要求Anthropic签署协议允许Claude用于大规模监控和全自动武器，CEO Dario Amodei以模型不可靠为由拒绝。五角大楼将其列为"供应链风险"，起诉未果。OpenAI调整立场获得订单。Polymarket预测6月底前和解概率仅9%。此事件凸显AI公司需在原则与政府合作间抉择。

trendEntities.title: Qwen-RobotManip：对齐解锁机器人操作基础模型的规模化能力
normalizedKeys: title:qwen robotmanip 对齐解锁机器人操作基础模型的规模化能力

items.title: Qwen-RobotManip：对齐解锁机器人操作基础模型的规模化能力
items.summary: Qwen-RobotManip 是通义千问基于 Qwen-VL 的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）基础模型，引入覆盖表示、运动和行为三维度的统一对齐框架。仅使用开源机器人数据集和人演示视频，构建约 38，100 小时预训练语料，涵盖 15 种机器人形态。在 LIBERO-Plus 达 91.4%，RoboTwin-C2R Hard 达 69.4%，RoboCasa365 Composite-Unseen 达 14.9%，EBench 达 45.6%，RoboTwin-IF 达 72.0%，并在 RoboChallenge Table30 v1 generalist track 夺冠。模型采用 80 维状态-动作表示、人-机器人数据合成管道（1，933 小时第一人称视频转 24，808 小时数据）及上下文策略适配。

trendEntities.title: Qwen-RobotWorld：具身智能体的无界世界
normalizedKeys: title:qwen robotworld 具身智能体的无界世界

items.title: Qwen-RobotWorld：具身智能体的无界世界
items.summary: Qwen-RobotWorld以语言为统一动作接口，采用双流Multimodal Diffusion Transformer（MMDiT）架构，将Qwen2.5-VL作为动作编码器。在4个基准测试中取得顶尖成绩，统一20余种机器人形态，基于860万跨场景训练对和1300多项操作技能。语言接口标准化500多种动作类别，支持操作、自动驾驶、室内导航的联合训练。还支持Scene2Robot人类到机器人转移及2-4路多视角几何一致视频生成。

trendEntities.title: Qwen-RobotNav：面向智能体导航系统的可扩展导航模型
normalizedKeys: title:qwen robotnav 面向智能体导航系统的可扩展导航模型

items.title: Qwen-RobotNav：面向智能体导航系统的可扩展导航模型
items.summary: Qwen 发布 Qwen-RobotNav，基于 Qwen3-VL 在 15.6M 样本上训练，统一了视觉语言导航、目标导航、目标跟踪、自动驾驶和具身问答五个领域，无需修改架构即可在推理时切换任务模式和观察参数。模型在多项基准取得 SOTA：VLN-CE RxR 成功率 76.5%，HM3Dv2 目标导航 75.6%（仅 RGB），EVT-Bench 跟踪率 90.0%，NAVSIM PDMS 91.4，以及三项 EQA 新标杆。模型暴露四个可调轴（视觉 token 预算、时间衰减、相机权重、帧采样模式）。作为智能体系统的一部分，上层规划器 Qwen3.7-Plus 在 EXPRESS-Bench 上提升 15.4%，导航步数减少 77%。模型已零样本部署在 Unitree Go2 四足机器人上，无需环境微调。

trendEntities.title: 项目负责人揭秘为何苹果 AI 版 Siri 姗姗来迟：推倒重来，彻底重构
normalizedKeys: title:项目负责人揭秘为何苹果 ai 版 siri 姗姗来迟 推倒重来 彻底重构

items.title: 项目负责人揭秘为何苹果 AI 版 Siri 姗姗来迟：推倒重来，彻底重构
items.summary: 苹果AI版Siri迟迟未上线，项目负责人迈克·罗克韦尔在WWDC技术分享会上透露，去年团队曾做出在原有Siri基础上小幅改良、新增工具调用的可运行版本，但因无法达到产品愿景，最终选择推倒重来，完整从零重构系统，依托全新大模型搭建。重构后的Siri拥有独立应用程序，原生支持多模态交互，隐私保护贯穿底层架构，并覆盖iPhone、iPad、Mac、Apple Watch、Vision Pro、CarPlay、AirPods等全平台，提供统一连贯体验。

trendEntities.title: Meta 在 Facebook 上线"AI Mode"，基于平台公开信息合成答案
normalizedKeys: title:meta 在 facebook 上线 ai mode 基于平台公开信息合成答案

items.title: Meta 在 Facebook 上线"AI Mode"，基于平台公开信息合成答案
items.summary: Meta 宣布在 Facebook 推出"AI Mode"搜索功能，利用 Meta AI 从公开帖子（含群组和 Reels）提取信息并合成答案，用户可用自然语言提问获得摘要。同时新增视频拼贴剪辑、过渡效果及 AI 照片预设（可更换服装、发型和配饰），体育迷可在 Stories 中点击"AI Edit"虚拟穿上队服。这些更新延续了此前动态头像、Marketplace 自动回复和创作者 AI 助手的部署节奏。此外，Meta 近期启动了 Facebook、Instagram 和 WhatsApp 的全球订阅计划（每月 3.99 美元起），更多 AI 订阅层级正在规划中。

trendEntities.title: 下一代投机解码：DFlash 与 Spec V2
normalizedKeys: title:下一代投机解码 dflash 与 spec v2

items.title: 下一代投机解码：DFlash 与 Spec V2
items.summary: Z Lab、Modal 与 SGLang 团队联合发布 DFlash 投机解码模型和 SGLang 的默认 Spec V2 引擎。DFlash 采用块扩散+KV 注入并行生成整块 draft token，在 Qwen 3.5 397B-A17B（BF16）的 HumanEval 数据集上、并发 1 时吞吐量达到基线的 4.3

trendEntities.title: 免费LLM API比较：速率限制、模型与真实成本（2026）
normalizedKeys: title:免费llm api比较 速率限制 模型与真实成本 2026

items.title: 免费LLM API比较：速率限制、模型与真实成本（2026）
items.summary: 13个平台提供免费LLM API，含永久免费层与试用额度。OpenRouter拥有20+免费模型，单密钥无需信用卡；Groq以约320 tokens/秒运行Llama 3.3 70B；Google AI Studio支持1M上下文；Mistral实验层约10亿token/月但需同意数据训练；Cerebras约1M token/天；GitHub Models提供GPT-4o、Claude 3.5 Sonnet等前沿模型。各免费层有速率限制、数据训练授权、上下文缩减等隐藏成本，建议早期测试2-3个方案并设置故障转移。

trendEntities.title: 智能体AI治理：你的API密钥就是护栏
normalizedKeys: title:智能体ai治理 你的api密钥就是护栏

items.title: 智能体AI治理：你的API密钥就是护栏
items.summary: 智能体AI使用量将在未来两年急剧上升，但治理滞后--仅五分之一企业拥有成熟治理模型。典型事故：销售智能体重试失败调用后自行升级到GPT-5.5，一夜消耗200美元。API路由层位于智能体与模型之间，是强制执行预算上限、模型白名单、提供方准入和请求日志的理想点。IBM报告指出97%遭遇AI安全事件的组织缺乏AI访问控制。最小可行治理方案：为每个智能体工作流分配独立API密钥，在API密钥层面实现预算控制、模型准入和审计追踪。

trendEntities.title: MiniMax 开源 M3 模型权重及 MSA 技术论文
normalizedKeys: title:minimax 开源 m3 模型权重及 msa 技术论文

items.title: MiniMax 开源 M3 模型权重及 MSA 技术论文
items.summary: MiniMax 上周五开源了 428B 总参数、23B 激活参数的 M3 模型权重，同步发布 MSA（MiniMax Sparse Attention）技术论文，该架构显著降低长上下文计算成本。M3 是首个从预训练阶段就进行文本、图像等多模态交错混合训练的开源模型。发布两周后，M3 在 Artificial Analysis 综合智能指数、GDPval-AA 排行榜均获开源模型第一，Code Arena WebDev 跻身帕累托最优序列，Vals.AI 榜单居国产模型首位。输出速度已从约 30 TPS 提升至约 80 TPS，计划再提速 30-40%；Token Plan 后台新增调用量看板。

trendEntities.title: Salesforce以36亿美元收购AI客服平台Fin
normalizedKeys: title:salesforce以36亿美元收购ai客服平台fin

items.title: Salesforce以36亿美元收购AI客服平台Fin
items.summary: Salesforce宣布以36亿美元收购AI客服平台Fin（前身为Intercom）。Fin提供可跨实时聊天、WhatsApp、短信、电话、Slack等多渠道解决客户问题的AI智能体。Salesforce计划利用Fin的技术和团队增强其企业级Agentforce平台，该平台允许企业构建自定义AI智能体以自动化任务。交易预计在Salesforce 2027财年第四季度（即2027年初）完成。Fin联合创始人兼CEO Eoghan McCabe将继续担任CEO，研发负责人Des继续领导研发。

trendEntities.title: Skydio CEO Adam Bry：硅谷不应为无人机使用画红线
normalizedKeys: title:skydio ceo adam bry 硅谷不应为无人机使用画红线

items.title: Skydio CEO Adam Bry：硅谷不应为无人机使用画红线
items.summary: Skydio是美国最大的无人机制造商，主攻公共安全、军事、能源、基建巡检等企业市场。CEO Adam Bry表示，特朗普政府去年底禁止中国产无人机后，廉价消费级无人机几乎消失，Skydio产品成为主要替代方案。公司认为无人机正从工具转向自主基础设施--通过机库、远程操控和软件整合实现规模化应用，AI在其中扮演关键角色。访谈还涉及Skydio与军方合作的态度，以及自主技术如何带动公司扩张。

trendEntities.title: 6倍速！Kimi K2.7 Code 高速版已上线
normalizedKeys: title:6倍速 kimi k2 7 code 高速版已上线

items.title: 6倍速！Kimi K2.7 Code 高速版已上线
items.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code 高速版上线，与普通版为同一模型，输出速度约 5-6 倍，常规编程场景约 180 Token/s，短上下文可达 260 Token/s。API 定价为普通版 2 倍，模型 ID：kimi-k2.7-code-highspeed。Kimi Code Plan 用户可通过「抢先体验计划」使用，用量消耗为普通版 3 倍。使用须开启思考模式，关闭会报错或回退至 K2.6。庆祝发布，Kimi API 开放平台推出为期三周充赠活动，充值 500 元及以上享 20%-30% 代金券。相比 K2.6，K2.7 Code 在长上下文编程指令遵循、长程任务性能提升，平均 token 消耗减少 30%，内部基准测试显著提升。普通版输入 6.5 元/百万 token、输出 27 元，缓存输入 1.3 元。非编程任务推荐 K2.6。

trendEntities.title: Flash-KMeans：IO感知的精确K-Means，在GPU上比FAISS快200倍以上
normalizedKeys: title:flash kmeans io感知的精确k means 在gpu上比faiss快200倍以上

items.title: Flash-KMeans：IO感知的精确K-Means，在GPU上比FAISS快200倍以上
items.summary: UC Berkeley与UT Austin团队开源Flash-KMeans（Apache 2.0，`pip install flash-kmeans`），精确实现标准Lloyd's k-Means，通过重构GPU数据流而非改变数学或近似来提速。在NVIDIA H200上，端到端速度比最佳基线快17.9×，比cuML快33×，比FAISS快200×以上。其FlashAssign核避免物化完整N×K距离矩阵，将IO复杂度从O（NK）降至O（Nd+Kd），单核加速最高21.2×；Sort-Inverse Update核通过排序聚类ID减少原子争用，单核加速最高6.3×。支持out-of-core处理，在1B数据点、K=32768时单次迭代仅41.4s。适用于向量搜索索引、稀疏注意力路由、KV缓存压缩等在线场景。

trendEntities.title: AI裁员浪潮成为火药桶

items.title: AI裁员浪潮成为火药桶
items.summary: 今年科技公司已累计裁员约15万人，日均974人，速度比去年快44%；上月裁员近4万创两年新高，AI连续三个月被列为裁员首要原因。Block近半数员工被裁后，CEO Jack Dorsey否认AI是根源，Marc Andreessen则称AI只是"银弹借口"。Uber裁撤23%人事部门，但此前CTO透露AI编码预算四个月内耗尽。与此同时，AI芯片商Cerebras上市首日市值达670亿美元，SpaceX上市市值2.1万亿美元，Anthropic和OpenAI估值均约1万亿美元。Meta在扎克伯格购入1.7亿美元豪宅后宣布裁员8000人。民调显示65%选民认为中产阶级生活遥不可及，76%美国人将生活成本列为首要经济问题。

trendEntities.title: AI 应用黄金时代已至：Fable 被禁、Nadella 的护城河论点与 Salesforce 收购 Fin
normalizedKeys: title:ai 应用黄金时代已至 fable 被禁 nadella 的护城河论点与 salesforce 收购 fin

items.title: AI 应用黄金时代已至：Fable 被禁、Nadella 的护城河论点与 Salesforce 收购 Fin
items.summary: 美国政府关闭 Anthropic 的 Fable 访问，开源和本地模型成必备；Satya Nadella 主张 AI 生态护城河应是人类专业知识和模型外围系统；Salesforce 以 36 亿美元收购 Fin（前 Intercom），Fin 利用开源模型实现性价比。这三件事标志 AI 应用进入黄金时代。构建 AI 应用的难点：在 Kimi K2.6、Qwen 3.6 27b、GLM 5.1 等不同特性模型中选择；设计智能体系统的 hill-climbing 循环；持续评估模型+循环性能以最大化 token 预算中的智能。掌握这三项技能的公司将主导这一时代。

items.title: Grok Build 推出 Agent Dashboard 管理多个编码会话
items.summary: xAI 为 Grok Build 推出 Agent Dashboard，提供单一屏幕管理多个编码会话。仪表板按状态分组（等待输入、工作中、空闲），每行显示状态标记、名称、分支、权限模式和当前操作。选中会话可打开 peek 面板查看最新输出并直接回复，等待输入的会话支持用箭头键或数字键选择选项。底部输入框用于分派新会话，支持设置模型、启动计划模式或自动批准编辑。通过 `grok dashboard`、`/dashboard` 或 `Ctrl+\` 打开，关闭后会话继续运行，重新打开即可恢复。

trendEntities.title: OpenAI 推出合作伙伴网络 OpenAI Partner Network
normalizedKeys: title:openai 推出合作伙伴网络 openai partner network

items.title: OpenAI 推出合作伙伴网络 OpenAI Partner Network
items.summary: OpenAI 宣布推出 OpenAI Partner Network，并投资 1.5 亿美元支持全球合作伙伴构建、销售和交付 AI 解决方案。该计划设立 Select、Advanced、Elite 三级合作伙伴层级，提供 Codex、网络安全、智能体等专业方向认证，并试点 Forward Deployed Experts 项目以支持复杂企业部署。目标在 2026 年底前培训认证 30 万名顾问。案例显示，Paychex 借助 Bain 和 OpenAI 的方案将等待时间降低 80%，人工审核时间减少 30%。
items.description: OpenAI 宣布推出 OpenAI Partner Network，并投资 1.5 亿美元支持全球合作伙伴构建、销售和交付 AI 解决方案。该计划设立 Select、Advanced、Elite 三级合作伙伴层级，提供 Codex、网络安全、智能体等专业方向认证，并试点 Forward Deployed Experts 项目以支持复杂企业部署。目标在 2026 年底前培训认证 30 万名顾问。案例显示，Paychex 借助 Bain 和 OpenAI 的方案将等待时间降低 80%，人工审核时间减少 30%。

trendEntities.title: 白宫AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI与亚马逊
normalizedKeys: title:白宫ai监管决定被指偏袒openai与亚马逊

items.title: 白宫AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI与亚马逊
items.summary: 白宫周五做出的AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI、亚马逊等企业，同时对Anthropic施压不足24小时，缺乏透明度和事实依据。Gary Marcus、Dean W Ball及卡托研究所Kevin Frazier等专家指出，这种由少数人闭门快速决策的做法带有腐败嫌疑，可能促使其他国家加速发展"主权AI"甚至中国AI，并导致美国人才流失。Anthropic声明称政府应在法定程序中基于技术事实阻止不安全部署，而非当前方式。Marcus呼吁建立独立机构负责AI监管，确保公平、清晰、基于证据的执行。

The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
normalizedKeys: title:the value axis language models encode whether they re on the right track

items.title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
items.description: We investigate whether language models internally track the value of their current trajectory, defined as the likelihood that their ongoing strategy will achieve their goals. Using synthetic, in-context reinforcement learning data, we construct a "value" axis for Qwen3-8B. We find that activations along this axis distinguish between high vs. low verbalized confidence, rollouts without and with backtracking, and correct vs. corrupted code. Steering towards high value causally suppresses self-corr
items.summary: We investigate whether language models internally track the value of their current trajectory, defined as the likelihood that their ongoing strategy will achieve their goals. Using synthetic, in-context reinforcement learning data, we construct a "value" axis for Qwen3-8B. We find that activations along this axis distinguish between high vs. low verbalized confidence, rollouts without and with backtracking, and correct vs. corrupted code. Steering towards high value causally suppresses self-correction and reduces explanatory verbosity, while steering towards low value induces backtracking and exploration. We demonstrate that direct preference optimization (DPO) can increase the internal value of rewarded behaviors (e.g. use a certain word), causing the model to act more confidently after exhibiting them. Finally, we apply the value axis to study in-the-wild settings. For example, we find that Qwen assigns low value to politically sensitive chat queries after post-training and that supervised fine-tuning increases internal confidence within the training domain. Our results suggest that language models linearly encode an estimate of expected goal success that modulates their confidence in pursuing a direction.
items.author: Nick Jiang, Isaac Kauvar, Jack Lindsey
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: fine-tuning

items.title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
items.description: Large language models (LLMs) often fail when answering requires identifying a small but decisive piece of evidence within a long or complex context, such as a single line in a tool trace or a subtle detail in an image. We propose ContextRL, a context-aware reinforcement learning (RL) method that improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance through an \emph{indirect} auxiliary objective. items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) often fail when answering requires identifying a small but decisive piece of evidence within a long or complex context, such as a single line in a tool trace or a subtle detail in an image. We propose ContextRL, a context-aware reinforcement learning (RL) method that improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance through an \emph{indirect} auxiliary objective. Instead of supervising only the final answer, ContextRL presents the model with a query, an answer, and two highly similar contexts, and rewards it for selecting the context that supports the query--answer pair, thereby encouraging fine-grained grounding. We construct contrastive context data in two domains: for coding agents, trajectories serve as contexts, yielding 1k pairs built via condition filtering; for multimodal reasoning, images serve as contexts, yielding 7K pairs built via generative editing and similarity search. ContextRL achieves average gains of +2.2% over standard GRPO on 5 long-horizon benchmarks, and +1.8% across 12 diverse visual question answering benchmarks. To disentangle the effect of the proposed objective from that of additional data, we compare against data-augmentation baselines that repurpose the same contrastive contexts as standard query--context--answer examples. These baselines provide little to no improvement, showing that the gains arise from the proposed context-selection objective rather than from the contrastive data alone.
items.author: Peiyang Xu, Bangzheng Li, Sijia Liu, Karthik R. Narasimhan, Pramod Viswanath, Prateek Mittal, Xingyu Fu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, rag, coding agent

trendEntities.title: Exact Posterior Score Estimation for Solving Linear Inverse Problems
normalizedKeys: title:exact posterior score estimation for solving linear inverse problems

items.title: Exact Posterior Score Estimation for Solving Linear Inverse Problems
items.description: Diffusion and flow-based models learn powerful data priors by training a denoiser to reverse Gaussian corruption. To use this prior to solve a linear inverse problem, one needs to sample from the posterior, but the score that the prior provides is the unconditional score, not the posterior score. Existing methods either steer a fixed pretrained denoiser with approximate measurement-matching corrections, or train a conditional restoration model that abandons the denoising structure of the prior.
items.summary: Diffusion and flow-based models learn powerful data priors by training a denoiser to reverse Gaussian corruption. To use this prior to solve a linear inverse problem, one needs to sample from the posterior, but the score that the prior provides is the unconditional score, not the posterior score. Existing methods either steer a fixed pretrained denoiser with approximate measurement-matching corrections, or train a conditional restoration model that abandons the denoising structure of the prior. We derive the exact posterior score in closed form for linear Gaussian inverse problems under general Gaussian interpolants, and show that posterior sampling reduces to a denoising problem at an operator-dependent shifted pivot under an anisotropic noise covariance. We turn this identity into Exact Posterior Score (EPS), a denoising training objective that preserves the input/output structure of standard pretraining and can therefore be trained from scratch or fine-tuned from a pretrained denoiser. At inference, EPS uses the same sampler as the underlying backbone, with no likelihood gradients or projections. We evaluate EPS on five linear inverse problems across FFHQ and ImageNet, where it outperforms training-free and training-based baselines on fidelity, perceptual, and distributional metrics, while using roughly an order of magnitude fewer denoiser evaluations than gradient-based posterior samplers.
items.author: Abbas Mammadov, Ozgur Kara, Kaan Oktay, Iskander Azangulov, Adil Kaan Akan, Hyungjin Chung, James Matthew Rehg, Yee Whye Teh
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: evaluation

We derive the exact posterior score in closed form for linear Gaussian inverse problems under general Gaussian interpolants, and show that posterior sampling reduces to a denoising problem at an operator-dependent shifted pivot under an anisotropic noise covariance. We turn this identity into Exact Posterior Score (EPS), a denoising training objective that preserves the input/output structure of standard pretraining and can therefore be trained from scratch or fine-tuned from a pretrained denoiser. At inference, EPS uses the same sampler as the underlying backbone, with no likelihood gradients or projections. We evaluate EPS on five linear inverse problems across FFHQ and ImageNet, where it outperforms training-free and training-based baselines on fidelity, perceptual, and distributional metrics, while using roughly an order of magnitude fewer denoiser evaluations than gradient-based posterior samplers.

items.title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
items.description: Generalist robot policies must follow user instructions while reasoning about how objects, cameras, and robot actions interact in the 3D physical world. Recent vision-language-action models (VLAs) and video world-action models (WAMs) inherit strong semantic or temporal priors from large-scale foundation models, but they still operate primarily on 2D image frames or 2D-derived latent spaces, leaving implicit the 3D geometry required for contact-rich manipulation. We propose the Geometric Action M
items.summary: Generalist robot policies must follow user instructions while reasoning about how objects, cameras, and robot actions interact in the 3D physical world. Recent vision-language-action models (VLAs) and video world-action models (WAMs) inherit strong semantic or temporal priors from large-scale foundation models, but they still operate primarily on 2D image frames or 2D-derived latent spaces, leaving implicit the 3D geometry required for contact-rich manipulation. We propose the Geometric Action Model (GAM), a language-conditioned manipulation policy that directly repurposes a pretrained geometric foundation model (GFM) as a shared substrate for perception, temporal prediction, and action decoding. GAM splits the GFM at an intermediate layer: the shallow layers serve as an observation encoder, and a causal future predictor inserted at the split layer forecasts future latent tokens conditioned on language, proprioception, and action history. The predicted future tokens are then routed through the remaining GFM blocks for feature propagation and decoding, allowing a single backbone to produce both future geometry and actions. This design equips the GFM with language-conditioned temporal world modeling through minimal architectural modification while preserving its rich geometric priors. Across a broad suite of simulation and real-robot manipulation benchmarks, GAM is more accurate, more robust, faster, and lighter than current foundation-model-scale baselines.
items.author: Jisang Han, Seonghu Jeon, Jaewoo Jung, René Zurbrügg, Honggyu An, Tifanny Portela, Marco Hutter, Marc Pollefeys, Seungryong Kim, Sunghwan Hong

items.title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes
items.description: When pretrained VLA policies are fine-tuned through online RL, each rollout episode produces only a single binary outcome (success or failure), yet the actor update requires per-transition supervision. Existing approaches commonly reduce this sparse outcome to a single scalar reward or advantage signal, which conflates distinct forms of transition-level feedback and provides limited guidance once basic task success becomes achievable. First, a single scalar signal conflates the two objectives of
items.summary: When pretrained VLA policies are fine-tuned through online RL, each rollout episode produces only a single binary outcome (success or failure), yet the actor update requires per-transition supervision. Existing approaches commonly reduce this sparse outcome to a single scalar reward or advantage signal, which conflates distinct forms of transition-level feedback and provides limited guidance once basic task success becomes achievable. First, a single scalar signal conflates the two objectives of viability and efficiency; once basic success is achieved, the binary label provides no gradient to distinguish efficient completions from slow ones. Second, real-world rollouts mix autonomous and intervention segments; naively assigning episode outcomes across these boundaries introduces incorrect credit assignment. To address these issues, we propose Hierarchical Advantage-Weighted Behavior Cloning (HABC), which trains separate critic heads for these two objectives on different data subsets and combines their outputs with a state-adaptive balance. A state-adaptive gate $g_t$ merges their one-step advantages, prioritizing viability when success is uncertain and shifting to efficiency only when viability is high, and converts the result into per-transition weights on the actor loss. Intervention-aware credit assignment further restricts outcome labels to segments executed by the current policy, preventing supervision from leaking across intervention boundaries. In real-robot experiments on three contact-rich bimanual tasks, HABC raises success from supervised fine-tuning (SFT) baselines of 36%, 44%, and 12% to 92%, 88%, and 38%.
items.author: Tongyan Fang, Siyuan Huang, Naiyu Fang, Ganlong Zhao, Zhongjin Luo, Jianbo Liu, Xiaogang Wang, Ying Dong, Hongsheng Li
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: fine-tuning

trendEntities.title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio

items.title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
items.description: Meta-analysis is a demanding form of evidence synthesis that combines literature retrieval, PI/ECO-guided study selection, and statistical aggregation. Its structured, verifiable workflow makes it an ideal substrate for evaluating systematic scientific reasoning, yet existing benchmarks lack ground truth across the full retrieval-screening-synthesis pipeline. We introduce MetaSyn, a dataset of 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio journals. Each entry pairs a research question w
items.summary: Meta-analysis is a demanding form of evidence synthesis that combines literature retrieval, PI/ECO-guided study selection, and statistical aggregation. Its structured, verifiable workflow makes it an ideal substrate for evaluating systematic scientific reasoning, yet existing benchmarks lack ground truth across the full retrieval-screening-synthesis pipeline. We introduce MetaSyn, a dataset of 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio journals. Each entry pairs a research question with PI/ECO criteria, a retrieval corpus of 140k PubMed articles, verified positive studies, hard negatives that are topically similar but PI/ECO-ineligible, and complete search strategies and date bounds. Benchmarking twelve pipeline configurations (nine RAG variants and a protocol-driven agent) reveals a critical screening bottleneck: despite a retrieval ceiling of 90.9% recall at K=200, no system recovers more than 52.7% of ground-truth included literature. Current LLMs fail to reliably separate eligible studies from PI/ECO-failing distractors in pools of comparable topical relevance. Stage-attributed metrics capture where systems succeed and fail; a single end-to-end score does not.
items.author: Anzhe Xie, Weihang Su, Yujia Zhou, Yiqun Liu, Qingyao Ai
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag, benchmark, workflow

trendEntities.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
normalizedKeys: title:the importance of phase in neural representations an internal oppenheim lim test of image classifiers

items.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
items.description: Oppenheim and Lim (1981) showed that natural images stay recognizable when reconstructed from their Fourier phase alone, while the magnitude carries little of their identity. We ask whether trained image classifiers reproduce this asymmetry inside their hidden layers, and we test it causally: given two images, we transplant the phase of one onto the magnitude of the other at a chosen layer and record which image the prediction follows. In PRISM2D, GFNet, and ViT-B/16 the prediction follows the p
items.summary: Oppenheim and Lim (1981) showed that natural images stay recognizable when reconstructed from their Fourier phase alone, while the magnitude carries little of their identity. We ask whether trained image classifiers reproduce this asymmetry inside their hidden layers, and we test it causally: given two images, we transplant the phase of one onto the magnitude of the other at a chosen layer and record which image the prediction follows. In PRISM2D, GFNet, and ViT-B/16 the prediction follows the phase or sign donor, and deleting all image-specific magnitude barely moves accuracy, so identity rides on phase while image-specific magnitude is largely dispensable to the readout. ResNet-50 at first seems to break the pattern, because transplanting sign after its ReLUs does nothing; a fair intervention before the ReLU reveals a strong latent sign code in the late blocks, and a DC-only control shows the readout consumes a channel-wise spatial average. Controls rule out the trivial case in which magnitude simply stops depending on the image. The architectures therefore share a phase/sign identity code but expose it in different bases, set by rectification and readout geometry, which gives a mechanistic account of the texture--shape gap between CNNs and attention models.
items.author: Alper Yıldırım

trendEntities.title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
normalizedKeys: title:your privacy my cloak backdoor attacks on differentially private federated learning

items.title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
items.description: Prior research suggests that differential privacy (DP) inherently enhances the robustness of federated learning (FL) against backdoor attacks. In this paper, we challenge this assumption. Through an empirical analysis of two baseline attack strategies, we uncover a fundamental tension in DP-FL: while bypassing DP allows state-of-the-art defenses to detect and filter malicious updates, complying with DP inadvertently masks their distinguishing statistical characteristics. Consequently, existing d
items.summary: Prior research suggests that differential privacy (DP) inherently enhances the robustness of federated learning (FL) against backdoor attacks. In this paper, we challenge this assumption. Through an empirical analysis of two baseline attack strategies, we uncover a fundamental tension in DP-FL: while bypassing DP allows state-of-the-art defenses to detect and filter malicious updates, complying with DP inadvertently masks their distinguishing statistical characteristics. Consequently, existing defenses become ineffective as DP reduces the raw backdoor signal. Building on this masking effect, we propose RING, a novel attack that explicitly exploits DP to conceal malicious contributions while maximizing attack impact. By collaboratively crafting adversarial perturbations, compromised clients reconstruct a strong backdoor signal during aggregation without triggering anomaly detection. RING operates as a perturbation layer that is agnostic to the underlying backdoor technique, making it broadly applicable and composable with existing attacks -- a property that significantly amplifies the threat it poses to DP-FL. Extensive evaluations across four image and text datasets under non-iid distributions show that RING achieves an average attack success rate of 90.3% against six state-of-the-art defenses under a moderate privacy budget, an improvement of up to 26.08x over baseline strategies. Finally, we evaluate potential countermeasures and find that mitigating this threat incurs significant utility trade-offs, exposing a fundamental security gap in the deployment of differentially private FL.
items.author: Xiaolin Li, Ning Wang, Ninghui Li, Wenhai Sun
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, evaluation

trendEntities.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing

items.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
items.description: Post-hoc context erasing over the KV cache is challenging because a local edit has a global consequence: once a span has been processed, its influence propagates into the cached states of all subsequent tokens. This issue arises naturally in long-context LLM applications, where stale retrieved facts, incorrect tool observations, retracted user preferences, or harmful prompt injections may be identified only after prefill. items.summary: Post-hoc context erasing over the KV cache is challenging because a local edit has a global consequence: once a span has been processed, its influence propagates into the cached states of all subsequent tokens. This issue arises naturally in long-context LLM applications, where stale retrieved facts, incorrect tool observations, retracted user preferences, or harmful prompt injections may be identified only after prefill. Exact erasing must then recompute all tokens after the deleted span, making its computational cost depend on suffix length rather than erased-span length. We introduce KVEraser, a learned KV-cache editing method for efficient localized context erasing. Given a processed context and a span to remove, KVEraser replaces only the KV states of the erased interval with learned steering states while reusing the remaining cache unchanged. To learn a transferable erasing mechanism, we build a two-stage training pipeline: generic span-neighbor pre-training teaches the eraser to suppress the influence of the erased span, while task-specific fine-tuning adapts this capability to downstream scenarios. Experiments show that KVEraser nearly matches full recomputation in post-erasure performance on in-domain tasks across 1K--32K context lengths, while its latency increases by only 24% compared with a 17.6x increase for full recomputation. KVEraser also generalizes to unseen long-document QA tasks with harmful factual distractors, achieving the best performance among approximate baselines with a 3--4x speedup over full recomputation.
items.author: Mufei Li, Shikun Liu, Dongqi Fu, Haoyu Wang, Yinglong Xia, Hong Li, Hong Yan, Pan Li
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, fine-tuning
